posts.text:
Our Helper applications for the BES Annual Meeting 2026 are open 

Get the opportunity to meet new friends, make connections and get an insight into what goes into the biggest ecology conference in Europe! 

Apply by 10 August 👇

https://f.mtr.cool/rrqxpeqdmk

New collaborative paper led by Takehiro Sasaki out @natecoevo.nature.com with plenty of contributors from our group & others @idiv-research.bsky.social 

Biodiversity enhances productivity under drought in more arid grasslands but has limited effects in more arid forests 🌱🌲

doi.org/10.1038/s415...

posts.external_description: Biodiversity effects on ecosystem functioning under climate extremes may depend on ecosystem type and baseline aridity. Here the authors show that the effects of plant diversity on productivity are st...

posts.text: Compiling a global database of non-structural carbohydrates (NSCs) from >2,000 plant species, the authors show that evolutionary history explains a larger proportion of NSC variation than environmental controls 🧪 www.nature.com/articles/s41...

posts.external_description: Assessing the response of the South Asian monsoon to rapid climate change requires understanding its seasonal driving mechanisms under changing climate conditions. Now, advanced geochemical imaging of...

posts.text: Here the authors show that species’ range dynamics determine the ability of correlative and process-based models to predict extinction risk under climate change, and suggest how to update the IUCN guidelines accordingly 🧪 www.nature.com/articles/s41...
posts.external_title: Models used for Red List assessments underestimate climate-related extinction risk of range-shifting species - Nature Ecology & Evolution

posts.author_name: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
posts.text:
🌍 #SDG 13: Climate Action

A PNAS study finds that a new climate regime is restructuring the ecology of Canada's Northern Great Lakes, highlighting how peer-reviewed research can support progress toward the UN Sustainable Development Goals.

Read the article: https://ow.ly/fFf250ZmIi4

posts.author_name: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
posts.text: Opinion piece: #EnhancedRockWeathering removes #CO2 from the atmosphere. But does ERW really have more promise as a sustainable #farming practice than as a #CarbonDioxideRemoval technology? In PNAS Front Matter: https://ow.ly/ySg350ZmIgn
